title: pyrenacantha occidentalis
intro: Pyrenacantha occidentalis, also known as the Western Pyrenacantha, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, specifically in the regions of Cameroon and the Congo. It is a woody climber with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction to harness its medicinal properties.
